dialogos.texto_en: Ah! Uesugi-san! Come here!\nQuickly, quickly!
What's wrong? Did something happen?
Please listen!\nWe've just discovered a SHOCKING fact!
What is it?
Don't be too surprised, okay?\nDrumroll%p-2;――
Sea grapes aren't actually fruit grapes!!
I know, it's surprising, right?\nEven you probably thought they were fruit%p-2;――
I didn't.
Huh?
I think it's only you,\nYotsuba, who thought they were actual fruits.
EEEEHH?!
No matter how you look at it,\nthere are some obvious differences from real grapes.
Ah, that's not true!\nI didn't even know that the thing I was eating was sea grapes!
They were on a salad and we ate them without even realizing it.
Well, I thought they were some kind of grape\nexclusive to Okinawa.\nI was curious about where they were sold.
Well, I guess I can understand that.\nThey're called 'sea grapes' because they\nlook like grapes.
But now I understand it properly.\nSea grapes are vegetables.
No, they're actually seaweed.
...Huh?\nThey're not even vegetables?!
Nope. Seaweed.
So...those things that look like little balls,\nthey really just sway around in the sea like that?
Well, that's probably the case, right?\nI haven't seen where they grow, though.
That's kind of creepy.\nBumpy seaweed, swaying in the waves...
Um, it's not that creepy.\nSeaweed growing in the ocean is a common sight.
Why do you think that way, Yotsuba?
Because...\ndoesn't it look like some kind of egg?
Ooh...
Ah...\nI also imagine tapioca or something like that%p-2;――
Futaro, please don't say anymore.
The reason why we can enjoy eating things\nthat look scary is thanks to the person\nwho first put it in their mouth, right?
Yeah, that's true for sea grapes too.\nI'm impressed by the first people who ate things\nlike sea urchin and sea cucumber.
Octopus and squid might be the same.\nOctopuses are called demons in some foreign countries,\nyou know?
Well, I guess it's a little creepy.\nI don't think much of it by itself,\nbut when I imagine it wriggling around...
That's right! Doesn't it give you the creeps?
They're like some kind of egg%p-2;――
Hyaa!\nLet's not talk about that!
Now I'm imagining it too, because of you two...
The reason why we can enjoy eating things\nthat look scary is thanks to the person\nwho first put it in their mouth, right?
Yeah, that's true for sea grapes too.\nI'm impressed by the first people who ate things\nlike sea urchin and sea cucumber.
Octopus and squid might be the same.\nOctopuses are called demons in some foreign countries,\nyou know?
You're one to talk. You enjoyed scarfing 'em down.\nDidn't you say the texture was pleasant?
Ahaha...\nWell, that's a different story.
It's fun to pop them, isn't it?
Yeah, it is.\nI could probably eat a whole bucket of them.
...That's impossible.
You'd get tired of them eventually...
The reason why we can enjoy eating things\nthat look scary is thanks to the person\nwho first put it in their mouth, right?
Yeah, that's true for sea grapes too.\nI'm impressed by the first people who ate things\nlike sea urchin and sea cucumber.
Octopus and squid might be the same.\nOctopuses are called demons in some foreign countries,\nyou know?
Demonic octopus?\nYou must mean devilfish.
And, it spits ink too!\nIf you didn't know anything about it,\nyou'd be surprised.
But we should be grateful to our ancestors\nwho challenged themselves to eat it.
...Oh, speaking of sea grapes,\nI remember that Okinawans eat a lot of marine plants.
Really? That so?
Yeah, not just sea grapes,\nbut also mozuku algae and stuff.\nThey serve it at hotels and other restaurants too.
Ah, when you say it like that, there is a lot, huh.
Oh, come to think of it,\nthey sell mozuku tempura on Kokusai dori.\nI heard people eat it like a snack.
Like a snack, huh?\nIt must be really ingrained in their culture.
If you think about it,\nit makes sense that sea grapes are used in salads too.
Heheheh, I feel like I know a lot more\nabout sea grapes now. Maybe they'll taste even better\nnext time.
Well, the taste won't change, right?
Your feelings make it change a little!\nFrom now on, I'll think of it as eating seaweed.
...Oh, I see. You didn't even know\nit was sea grapes you were eating before.
Heheh, that's right.
Sea grapes are not grapes.\nThat might be the biggest lesson of this trip.
Heh. I feel like I've become smarter.
Whoa. Really?
